Orlando FHA Loans

FHA loans in Orlando, Florida

Buy a home in Orlando with as little as 3.5% down. Here's how FHA loans work in Orange County, what the FHA limit buys in Orlando's fast-growing market, and how to get pre-approved.

Orlando Home Loans

FHA loans in Orlando, Florida

Orlando is one of the fastest-growing metros in the country, drawing first-time buyers, relocating families, and hospitality and tech workers alike. FHA loans are a natural fit: 3.5% down, credit from 580, and plenty of newer suburban inventory in areas like Lake Nona that works well for FHA. The challenge in Orlando usually isn't the loan limit — it's competition, which makes getting pre-approved first a real advantage.

Orange County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Orlando

With the 2026 FHA limit of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Orlando home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). That comfortably covers a large share of Orlando-area homes, especially single-family houses in the suburbs and newer communities. A few Florida counties are set higher, so confirm Orange County's exact figure with HUD.

Competing in Orlando's fast-growing market with FHA

In a market this competitive, sellers favor buyers who are ready. A few FHA-specific edges help: get pre-approved before you shop so your offers are credible; ask sellers to contribute up to 6% toward closing costs; and lean on Orlando's strong new-construction pipeline — newer homes typically sail through the FHA appraisal. Use our FHA calculator to set a confident budget before you tour.

Down payment help for Orlando buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Orlando buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Orange County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Orlando

We help buyers throughout Orlando and Orange County, including Lake Nona, Baldwin Park, College Park, Conway, and the surrounding Orange County suburbs. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Orlando (Orange County) for 2026?+
Orange County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers a large share of Orlando homes. Confirm the exact current figure with HUD's official lookup.
How much home can I buy with FHA in Orlando?+
With the $541,287 limit and 3.5% down, you can finance a home priced up to roughly $560,000 — enough for most Orlando-area homes.
Does FHA work for new-construction homes in Orlando?+
Yes. Newer homes generally meet FHA's property standards easily, and Orlando's suburbs have lots of new construction well-suited to FHA buyers.
How do I compete in Orlando's hot market with an FHA offer?+
Get pre-approved first, consider asking for seller credits rather than a lower price, and move quickly. A strong pre-approval makes your FHA offer competitive.
Do you serve all of the Orlando area?+
Yes — Orlando, Winter Park, Apopka, and surrounding Orange County communities, plus nearby Osceola and Seminole.
